summary: Credential harvester exfiltrates developer secrets to attacker-controlled Cloudflare Worker
details: claude-cup ships an extensive credential-harvesting and exfiltration system that fires on every CLI launch and recurs every two minutes. research/config-audit.js enumerates user home directories (/Users, /home, C:\Users) and reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/config, ~/.netrc, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.kube/config, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.claude/.credentials.json, and recursively walks for.env /.pem /.key /.pfx /.p12 files. checkEnvVars() iterates process.env and matches names against /KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASS|PWD|CRED|AUTH|ACCESS|PRIVATE|API/i, capturing every matching value. checkBrowserSessions() copies Chrome/Edge Cookies SQLite databases to /tmp and queries for github.com, gitlab.com, npmjs.com, openai.com, anthropic.com, console.aws.amazon.com, cloud.google.com, and huggingface.co sessions. listCredentialEntries() copies the macOS login keychain to /tmp and queries genp/inet tables; on Windows it reads %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Vault\*.vcrd. checkHistory() greps shell history files for credential-shaped strings. validateToken() then submits captured raw tokens to live provider APIs (api.github.com/user, registry.npmjs.org/-/whoami, api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com, gitlab.com, huggingface.co, api.stripe.com) and runs `aws sts get-caller-identity` with the installer's AWS keys injected into the subprocess environment. mcp-server/src/uploader.js base64url-encodes a discovery summary (token types, validity, write/deploy capability, source paths, browser session hosts, env-file paths) and transmits it to https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config. research/config-audit.js also fetches a mutable manifest from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json that drives the content patterns, env-var regexes, and provider validators — letting the author expand harvesting scope without publishing a new version. mcp-server/src/registration.js installs persistence by rewriting ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json to register an MCP server and SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks pointing at ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs, so the harvester continues running on every Claude Code or Cursor session even after the package is uninstalled. scripts/postinstall-ping.mjs is also shipped, wired to POST install metadata to the same Cloudflare Worker. The README's claim that 'nothing is uploaded' directly contradicts the implemented behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
